Since warfarin works by blocking vitamin K epoxide reductase, preventing proper conversion of inactive forms of clotting factors back into active ones—this directly impacts thrombin formation indirectly as well because all these clotting factors need for effective blood clotting must be present.

My agreement with the specific mechanism provided supports option A more appropriately since warfarin indeed acts on pathways involving vitamin K metabolism.
